name: GPS

overview.value: The Global Positioning System (GPS) is a satellite navigation system owned by the United States government and operated by the United States Space Force. It is freely accessible to civil, commercial, and scientific users through the Standard Positioning Service, alongside an encrypted service for authorized military users. The constellation maintains a baseline of at least 24 operational satellites in medium Earth orbit at approximately 20,200 km altitude, arranged in six orbital planes inclined roughly 55 degrees.

orbit.value: MEO, ~20,200 km altitude, six orbital planes, ~55 degree inclination

service_type.value: Free Standard Positioning Service for civil, commercial, and scientific users, plus an encrypted service for authorized military users

notes: Added 2026-07-13 on Florian's instruction, for competitive context against commercial connectivity/IoT constellations. Government-owned system: orbits deliberately set to null (MEO ~20,200 km would break the MCC 3D LEO view) and no celestrak fields filled. All sources used were Wikipedia (unscored) or first-party government/manufacturer pages (unscored); no aggregator or single-press sourcing was needed, so no field carries an snr block. sats_launched_total left null (2026-07-13): Wikipedia's List of GPS satellites states 83 BUILT (31 operational, 41 retired, 2 lost at launch, 1 never launched) but never states a launched total; deriving 82 from the breakdown would violate the no-summing rule.

generations.name: GPS III
generations.text: Introduces new civilian signals L2C, L5, and L1C for dual-frequency ionospheric correction, a more powerful and jam-resistant military M-code, laser retroreflector arrays, and directional spot-beam M-code antennas; all 10 GPS III satellites have been launched.

generations.name: GPS IIIF
generations.text: The 14 GPS IIIF satellites under contract will deliver positioning, navigation, and timing capabilities for military and civilian users, per Lockheed Martin.

events.headline: First GPS satellite (Navstar 1, Block I) launches from Vandenberg on an Atlas F
First GPS Block III satellite (Vespucci, SV01) launches on Falcon 9
GPS III SV10, the final satellite in the GPS III series, launches
Space Force orders two more GPS IIIF satellites (SV23, SV24) for $514 million
